title: veronica media var. laxiflora
intro: Veronica media var. laxiflora, also known as the laxiflora broomwort or the loose-flowered broomwort, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It is used for its medicinal properties, particularly its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Veronica media var. laxiflora include reducing inflammation, soothing coughs, and easing respiratory congestion, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds and flu. This plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used as a dried herb for smoking to help clear mucus from the lungs.
